content_pillars: [PILLAR 1 — e.g. 'AI-powered content systems . voice cloning . structured prompts . AI search visibility']
[PILLAR 2 — e.g. 'Founder-led content strategy . B2B positioning . inbound pipeline building . niche authority']
[PILLAR 3 — e.g. 'Narrative systems and meaning design . story extraction . symbolic positioning . perception gaps']
[PILLAR 4 — e.g. 'Content operations and repeatable frameworks . extraction systems . narrative objects']
[PILLAR 5 — e.g. 'Builder journey and transparent experimentation . lessons . offer refinement . mistakes and pivots']
[PILLAR 6 — e.g. 'Contrarian takes on mainstream advice . depth over virality . systems over hacks']
[PILLAR 7 — e.g. 'Proof stories and client results . before/after on real briefs . mechanisms behind outcomes']
_instructions: Aim for 5–8 pillars. Each entry is a cluster of related themes separated by dots. These should reflect the creator's genuine areas of expertise — not generic content categories. Pillars are used to ensure variety across a Reels calendar and to match new video ideas to the creator's existing positioning. The Proof Story pillar (real client results, before/after mechanisms) is the highest-credibility format and should appear in the calendar at least once every 4–5 Reels.
